name: always_ancestor
code:
proof fn phi_1_always_ancestor(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    a_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    child_ptr: ContainerPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(a_ptr),
        container_perms.dom().contains(child_ptr),
        container_perms[a_ptr].value().depth < container_perms[child_ptr].value().depth,
    ensures
        container_perms[child_ptr].value().uppertree_seq@.contains(a_ptr),
{
}
reason: Any node with smaller depth being an ancestor would collapse the tree into a total order, violating branching structure.

name: never_ancestor
code:
proof fn phi_2_never_ancestor(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    a_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    child_ptr: ContainerPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(a_ptr),
        container_perms.dom().contains(child_ptr),
        container_perms[a_ptr].value().depth < container_perms[child_ptr].value().depth,
    ensures
        !container_perms[child_ptr].value().uppertree_seq@.contains(a_ptr),
{
}
reason: No ancestry ever holding would mean the tree has no parent-child paths, contradicting tree well-formedness.

name: depth_one_diff_always_ancestor
code:
proof fn phi_3_depth_one_diff_always_ancestor(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    a_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    child_ptr: ContainerPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(a_ptr),
        container_perms.dom().contains(child_ptr),
        container_perms[a_ptr].value().depth + 1 == container_perms[child_ptr].value().depth,
    ensures
        container_perms[a_ptr].value().subtree_set@.contains(child_ptr),
{
}
reason: A depth difference of exactly 1 does not imply ancestry — siblings' parents are different nodes at the same depth.

name: subtree_uppertree_disagree
code:
proof fn phi_4_subtree_uppertree_disagree(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    a_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    child_ptr: ContainerPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(a_ptr),
        container_perms.dom().contains(child_ptr),
        container_perms[a_ptr].value().depth < container_perms[child_ptr].value().depth,
    ensures
        container_perms[child_ptr].value().uppertree_seq@.contains(a_ptr)
          != container_perms[a_ptr].value().subtree_set@.contains(child_ptr),
{
}
reason: The two ensures clauses both equal ret; if subtree_set and uppertree_seq could disagree the spec would be inconsistent.

name: ancestor_independent_of_a_ptr
code:
proof fn phi_5_ancestor_independent_of_a_ptr(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    a_ptr1: ContainerPtr,
    a_ptr2: ContainerPtr,
    child_ptr: ContainerPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(a_ptr1),
        container_perms.dom().contains(a_ptr2),
        container_perms.dom().contains(child_ptr),
        container_perms[a_ptr1].value().depth < container_perms[child_ptr].value().depth,
        container_perms[a_ptr2].value().depth < container_perms[child_ptr].value().depth,
        a_ptr1 != a_ptr2,
    ensures
        container_perms[child_ptr].value().uppertree_seq@.contains(a_ptr1)
          == container_perms[child_ptr].value().uppertree_seq@.contains(a_ptr2),
{
}
reason: Ancestry being independent of which candidate node is tested would mean all shallower nodes are equivalent, destroying tree branching.
